name: First State Bank of Dequeen
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Arkansas offering online/mobile banking, checking, savings, and loan services across multiple county locations.
description_long: First State Bank of DeQueen is a traditional community bank operating in Arkansas with FDIC insurance backing. The bank maintains physical branches across Sevier, Howard, and Pike Counties, positioning itself as a local financial institution with regional presence. The bank emphasizes accessibility through both traditional branch banking and modern digital channels. Founded as a full-service bank, FSB DeQueen serves the Arkansas region with standard retail banking products. The bank offers comprehensive online and mobile banking platforms through their UniFI system and mobile app (available on iPhone, iPad, and Android). Core services include checking and savings accounts, loan products, bill pay, money transfers between accounts, eStatements, and debit card management. The bank has integrated Brella™ technology allowing customers to set debit card alerts, activate travel notifications, and remotely disable/enable cards. They support digital wallets including Apple Pay and Google Pay integration with their debit cards. FSB DeQueen differentiates itself through monthly debit card usage drawings and incentive programs. Their eStatement system allows 60-day access to electronic statements delivered as PDFs. The bank emphasizes convenience with 24/7 online account access and mobile-first banking capabilities. Their UniFI platform is marketed as a "new and improved" online banking experience, suggesting recent technology upgrades. As a regional community bank, FSB DeQueen serves primarily Arkansas residents within their branch footprint. The website provides limited information about specific loan products, interest rates, fee structures, or credit requirements. The scope appears narrower than large national banks but broader than some niche digital banks. Customers should contact specific branch locations for detailed product information, pricing, and eligibility requirements.
services: Account alerts via email and text
Debit cards with Apple Pay/Google Pay support
Brella™ card controls (travel notifications, remote enable/disable)
pros: FDIC insurance coverage backed by U.S. Government
UniFI online banking platform with full account management (transfers, bill pay, loan payments, alerts)
Mobile app with Brella™ card controls including travel notifications and remote card enable/disable
eStatements accessible for 60 days with secure electronic delivery
Digital wallet support for Apple Pay and Google Pay
Multiple branch locations across three Arkansas counties
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Sevier, Howard, and Pike Counties in Arkansas
Website lacks pricing information, fee schedules, interest rates, or product details
No information about minimum balance requirements, account types, or credit criteria
Recent data center migration mentioned suggests potential service interruptions
Limited transparency about loan products, approval process, or terms available

contact.address: 402 W Collin Raye Dr, De Queen, AR 71832

best_for: Arkansas residents within Sevier, Howard, or Pike County service areas
Customers seeking FDIC-insured banking with local branch access
Mobile-first users wanting advanced debit card controls and digital wallet integration
Individuals preferring paperless banking with eStatement delivery
diagnosis: First State Bank of DeQueen is best for Arkansas residents within their three-county service area seeking traditional banking with modern digital conveniences and local branch access. Primary caveat: the website provides minimal detail on products, pricing, fees, or eligibility requirements, requiring direct contact with branches for specific offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 402 W Collin Raye Dr, De Queen, AR 71832
